blocks.hero_h1: Import a 2020 Toyota Alphard G edition to NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: The 2020 Toyota Alphard G edition offers space, comfort and features that work well for NZ families and shuttle operators when sourced carefully from Japan.
proof_points: Importing Estimate — compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: Many NZ buyers look at the 2020 Alphard G edition for its three-row seating, sliding doors and quiet open-road manners. It handles school runs, airport transfers and long trips with ease, while still being manageable around town and in tight carparks.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes — from Japan auction to NZ road-ready
savings_block.body: With a 2020 Toyota Alphard G edition, your total spend is made up of the Japanese auction price, plus shipping and insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee. We show you the auction price first, then add each cost line by line so you can see how it becomes a NZ-landed, on-road figure before you commit to bidding.

cta_form.headline: Find your Alphard G edition — we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: Walk through a late-model Alphard G edition and see how the sliding doors, three-row seating and rear cabin space work in real life.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The 2020 Toyota Alphard G edition gives you a roomy cabin, flexible seating and the kind of comfort that makes long drives less tiring for everyone on board. Japanese-market examples are often well optioned, with features like power sliding doors and modern safety tech that suit NZ conditions. By importing, you can target specific interior layouts, mileage ranges and colours rather than taking whatever is on a local yard. For many buyers, it’s a practical way to get a late-model Toyota people mover without stepping into new-vehicle pricing.

buyer_persona.headline: Is this car right for you?
buyer_persona.body: You might be running a busy household, a shuttle service or a private transfer business where you regularly carry more than five passengers. Maybe you’ve outgrown a standard SUV and want something easier for older family members or clients to get in and out of. If you’re after space, comfort and a late-model Toyota that can handle mixed NZ driving, the Alphard G edition is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We’ve been helping Kiwis bring in vehicles from Japan since 2009, handling hundreds of cars across family, commercial and enthusiast segments. Our role is to explain the costs clearly, share the auction documents and give you an honest view of each option before you decide to bid. You deal with a real NZ-based team that understands local compliance and day-to-day use on our roads. From first enquiry through to handover, we keep you informed so there are fewer surprises along the way.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Lets you carry a larger family or shuttle passengers without needing a full-size bus.
Makes school runs, kerbside pick-ups and tight carparks easier and safer.
Simple to drive in stop–start city traffic and on longer open-road trips.
Known for durability and wide parts availability across NZ workshops.
Often comes with higher spec interiors and comfort features than NZ-new people movers of the same age.

cta_mid_page.headline: Ready to see real Alphard options from Japan?
cta_mid_page.subtext: Tell us what you’re after and we’ll come back with current auction examples and estimated landed costs.

models_comparison.intro: Here’s how different 2020 Alphard G edition options can vary when you’re looking at Japanese auctions.

rows.notes: You’re targeting 2020 build years, which typically meet current NZ entry standards if ESC and safety features are present. We confirm build date and spec from the auction sheet before recommending a car.

The 2020 Alphard range includes petrol four-cylinder and V6 engines, and in some trims hybrid options. For G edition petrol models, we confirm the exact engine size and whether it’s front-wheel drive or all-wheel drive so you can weigh performance against fuel use.

Within G edition listings you’ll see variations in seat material, power doors, alloy wheels, audio/navigation units and safety packages. Higher trims may add features like power tailgates or upgraded seating; we highlight these in each auction sheet summary.

2020 vehicles typically meet NZ entry standards for ESC and safety if documentation is clean and no major modifications are noted. We flag anything on the auction sheet that might affect entry certification, such as structural repairs or aftermarket suspension.

Alphard G edition models appear regularly at major Japanese auctions, but specific colours, mileages or option sets can be less common. If you’re picky on spec, you may need to wait a little longer for the right car to appear.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For a people mover like the Alphard, we pay close attention to seating layout, interior wear and any notes about commercial or shuttle use on the auction sheet.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common colour or trim combinations, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a 2020 Alphard G edition is straightforward, but it helps to understand the main risks and how we handle them.

items.risk: Worrying about buying a high-mileage or hard-used shuttle without realising it.
items.solution: We translate and review the auction sheet, looking for use history, odometer readings and interior wear notes, and talk you through what they mean before you decide to bid.

items.risk: Concern about hidden costs appearing after you’ve committed to a car.
items.solution: Before bidding, we give you an estimate that breaks out auction price, shipping, insurance, port charges, entry certification, compliance work and our fee so you can see where your money is going.

items.risk: Uncertainty about auction grades and what condition to expect on arrival.
items.solution: We explain the auction grade, note any repair history or panel comments, and share photos and documentation so you have a realistic picture of the vehicle’s condition.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: Start by telling us the year, budget and features you want in a 2020 Alphard G edition. We then watch the Japanese auctions, send you shortlisted cars with full cost estimates and only bid when you give the go-ahead. You stay in control of the decisions, and we handle the legwork and logistics.

faq_block.q: Is a 2020 Toyota Alphard G edition a good choice for a large family in NZ?
faq_block.a: For many families, the Alphard’s three-row seating, sliding doors and comfortable ride make day-to-day use easier than a standard SUV. It’s especially handy if you’re regularly carrying grandparents, kids and gear together.

faq_block.q: How much does it cost to import a 2020 Alphard G edition from Japan?
faq_block.a: Total cost depends on the auction price, mileage, spec level and shipping at the time. Your landed cost is made up of the hammer price, shipping and insurance, NZ port and MPI charges, entry certification, any compliance work and our fee.

faq_block.q: Will a 2020 Alphard G edition meet NZ entry standards?
faq_block.a: A 2020 Alphard typically meets NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no significant modifications are noted. Final entry certification is done in NZ, so we flag any auction sheet comments that might affect the process before you commit.

faq_block.q: How long does it take from winning an auction to driving the Alphard in NZ?
faq_block.a: From a successful bid, shipping usually takes around 2–4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule, then allow time for port processing, entry certification, compliance work and registration. In practice, most buyers see 6–10 weeks end to end.

faq_block.q: What does the auction grade mean for a 2020 Alphard?
faq_block.a: Auction grades give a quick view of overall condition, but the comments and diagrams are just as important. We go through the sheet with you, pointing out previous repairs, scratches, dents and interior wear so you know what to expect.

footer_cta.headline: Thinking about a 2020 Alphard G edition?
footer_cta.subtext: Tell us what you need in terms of seats, budget and features, and we’ll come back with real Japanese auction options and clear landed pricing.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get updates on fresh Alphard listings, price trends and practical import tips straight to your inbox.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based import service that’s been helping buyers source vehicles from Japan since 2009. We focus on clear pricing, full auction documentation and support through shipping and NZ entry. Our experience covers family people movers, commercial vehicles and specialist models.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost for a 2020 Alphard G edition includes the Japanese purchase price plus the key steps to get it on the road in NZ.

rows.notes: The hammer price you pay at the Japanese auction, based on the specific Alphard you choose.
Transport from the Japanese port to NZ and marine insurance to cover the vehicle in transit.
Port charges, MPI inspections, entry certification, any required compliance work and registration costs.

auction_checklist.check: Confirm auction grade and read all condition comments
auction_checklist.why: Gives you a realistic view of exterior and interior wear, previous repairs and how the Alphard has been treated.

auction_checklist.check: Check odometer reading and service stickers in photos
auction_checklist.why: Helps you spot unusually high use or gaps that might need more questions before bidding.

auction_checklist.check: Look closely at interior photos for seat wear and stains
auction_checklist.why: Many Alphards are used as shuttles; interior condition can reveal how hard a vehicle’s been worked.

auction_checklist.check: Confirm engine size, drivetrain and key features
auction_checklist.why: Ensures you’re getting the performance, fuel use and equipment level you expect for NZ driving.

auction_checklist.check: Review any notes on repairs or modifications
auction_checklist.why: Structural repairs or heavy modifications can affect NZ entry standards and long-term ownership costs.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: You want a compact vehicle that’s easy to park in very tight city streets or older garages.
You prefer a brand-new vehicle with local dealer support rather than an imported used car.
You rarely carry more than four people and don’t need the size and running costs of a large people mover.

alternatives_block.intro: If the 2020 Alphard G edition feels a bit large or outside your budget, there are other Japanese imports that might line up better with how you drive.

rows.better_if: You still need three rows of seats but want a slightly smaller, easier-to-park van.
rows.tradeoff: Less outright space and presence than an Alphard, but usually lower running costs.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain checks for the Alphard
engine_drivetrain_block.body: Because the 2020 Alphard G edition is a petrol people mover, it’s important to understand how the engine and transmission have been used. We look for signs of heavy shuttle use, towing or long idling, and pay attention to any engine bay photos and comments on oil leaks or noises. This helps you choose a vehicle that’s more likely to handle ongoing NZ use without early major repairs.
checklist: Confirm engine size and code from the auction sheet.
Check for comments about oil leaks, engine noise or warning lights.
Review transmission notes for slipping, harsh shifts or replacement history.
Look for signs of heavy commercial use that could mean more drivetrain wear.
Ask about any timing belt or major service history if noted.
